title: SpacetimeDB Schema Hydration on New Installs
driver: New hex installs need a zero-state SpacetimeDB with all 17 WASM module schemas, reducers, and procedures deployed — but no application data.
goal: hex nexus start (or hex stdb hydrate) on a fresh install should: discover/start SpacetimeDB, publish all 17 WASM modules in dependency order, verify schemas are live, run config_sync, and report health — fully automated, idempotent, no user data seeded.

architecture_context.spacetimedb_role: Backbone for all real-time state — agents, swarms, tasks, chat, inference, secrets, config. Connected via WebSocket from hex-nexus.
architecture_context.wasm_constraint: WASM modules cannot access filesystem, spawn processes, or make network calls. hex-nexus bridges that gap.
architecture_context.state_policy: ADR-025: SpacetimeDB is the ONLY state backend. SQLite removed. No fallback.
current_gaps: No module publish ordering — modules with cross-references can fail if published out of order
No schema validation after publish (is the table actually queryable?)
No reducer availability check (can we call swarm_init yet?)
No initial seed data (default inference providers, architecture rules)
No health check after config_sync (did sync_config succeed?)
hex init does not trigger SpacetimeDB setup at all

modules_dependency_order.description: Publish order based on cross-module references. Tier 0 has no deps, each tier depends on prior tiers.
tier_0_foundation: hexflo-coordination     — core tables: swarm, swarm_task, swarm_agent, hexflo_memory, project
tier_1_services: inference-gateway       — LLM request routing (references agent IDs)

tasks.title: Add `hex stdb hydrate` CLI command
tasks.description: New subcommand that orchestrates the full hydration pipeline. Calls hex-nexus REST endpoint to do the actual work. Flags: --host, --database, --force (re-publish even if modules exist), --dry-run.

tasks.title: Add POST /api/stdb/hydrate endpoint to hex-nexus
tasks.description: New route handler that accepts hydration request and orchestrates: (1) ensure SpacetimeDB is running, (2) publish modules in order, (3) verify schemas, (4) run config_sync, (5) return health report. Create new file hex-nexus/src/routes/stdb.rs for the handler.

tasks.title: Implement ordered module publisher in hex-nexus
tasks.description: Add a publish_modules_ordered() function that: (1) discovers all .wasm files in spacetime-modules/*/target/wasm32-unknown-unknown/release/, (2) sorts by the 4-tier dependency order defined above, (3) publishes each module via `spacetime publish` CLI, (4) waits for each tier to be live before publishing the next, (5) returns a manifest of published modules with status. Must be idempotent — skip modules already published at correct version.

tasks.title: Implement schema verification after publish
tasks.description: After each tier is published, verify: (1) tables exist and are queryable via WebSocket subscription, (2) key reducers are callable (dry-call with validation-only flag if available, or call a no-op reducer). Report any schema mismatches. This catches WASM compile issues where a module builds but its tables don't match expectations.

tasks.title: Embed WASM module binaries in hex-nexus at build time
tasks.description: Use rust-embed to bake pre-compiled WASM modules into the hex-nexus binary, similar to how dashboard assets and skill/agent templates are embedded. This allows hydration without requiring the spacetime-modules source tree. Add a WasmModules embed struct pointing to the compiled .wasm output directory. Build script (build.rs) should compile WASM modules before embedding.

tasks.title: Seed default architecture boundary rules
tasks.description: After modules are published, seed the architecture-enforcer module with the 8 standard hex boundary rules (domain imports only domain, ports import only domain, etc.) via the register_rule reducer. These rules must be present for hex analyze to work against SpacetimeDB. Idempotent — skip if rules already exist.

tasks.title: Enhance config_sync to report success/failure
tasks.description: Currently config_sync fires and forgets. Add return types that report: (1) number of configs synced, (2) any sync failures with reducer error messages, (3) timestamp of last successful sync. Store sync status in a SyncReport struct returned to the hydrate endpoint.

tasks.title: Add hydration health check endpoint
tasks.description: GET /api/stdb/health — returns a comprehensive health report: (1) SpacetimeDB connection status, (2) per-module publish status (version, table count, reducer count), (3) config_sync last run + status, (4) overall hydration state: 'empty' | 'partial' | 'hydrated' | 'stale'. Used by hex stdb status and the dashboard.

tasks.title: Wire hydration into hex nexus start
tasks.description: When hex-nexus starts and connects to SpacetimeDB, check hydration state. If 'empty', auto-run the hydration pipeline (publish modules, verify, seed, config_sync). If 'stale' (modules outdated), log a warning suggesting hex stdb hydrate --force. This makes first-time setup fully automatic.

tasks.title: Wire hydration into hex init
tasks.description: After hex init pulls templates from nexus, also trigger hydration if SpacetimeDB is available. Same graceful fallback pattern: if SpacetimeDB/nexus isn't running, print guidance. If running, hydrate and report module count in the summary.

acceptance_criteria: Fresh install: `hex nexus start` on empty SpacetimeDB → all 17 modules published, schemas verified, config synced, health reports 'hydrated'
Idempotent: running hydrate twice produces no errors and no duplicate data
No application state: hydration creates schemas + default rules only, zero swarms/tasks/agents/messages
Ordered publish: tier N modules only published after tier N-1 is verified live
Graceful offline: hex init without SpacetimeDB prints clear guidance, doesn't fail
Health observable: GET /api/stdb/health returns accurate per-module status
Dashboard integration: hydration status visible on the developer control plane
